title: Adapter sync flow: reactor ops encrypted, uploaded to Swarm, replayed on recovery
content:
End-to-end sync flow from document edit to cross-device recovery:

```
User edits document in Connect
       ↓
Reactor stores operation in local PGlite
       ↓
SyncManager detects new ordinal → populates SwarmChannel outbox
       ↓
SwarmChannel encrypts ops → uploads to Swarm /bytes → updates feed pointer
       ↓
On new device: wallet signature → same key → read feeds → download ops → replay → restored
```

Key properties:
- Local writes hit PGlite first, so edits never block on network I/O.
- The outbox is populated reactively by ordinal, not polled.
- Encryption happens at the SwarmChannel layer before `/bytes` upload.
- Feed pointers are the only mutable primitive — the op blobs themselves are immutable.
- Cross-device recovery is deterministic from the wallet signature alone, no backup seed phrase or extra credentials needed.
description: SyncManager detects new ordinals, SwarmChannel encrypts and uploads ops to /bytes and updates feed pointers; a new device replays ops after a wallet signature re-derives the key.
